title: The real reason AI progress feels so fast (and why it'll slow down)
post:
Every week brings another headline about AI breakthroughs. But here's what's actually happening: we're in the phase where easy gains are being captured. The low-hanging fruit of scaling transformers and feeding them more data has given us remarkably capable systems. But we're hitting physical and computational limits.

The next wave will be slower, harder, and require actual scientific breakthroughs — not just bigger models. That doesn't mean AI will stop improving. It means the rate of improvement will look different, and the companies that thrive will be the ones solving hard problems, not just scaling existing approaches.

Are you planning for AI's next chapter, or just reacting to the current one? Thoughts?
